title: The Bitbrain Open Access Sleep (BOAS) dataset

funding_references.funder_name: Spanish Ministry of Science and Innovation
funding_references.award_title: AI4HEALTHYAGING - Programa Misiones de I+D en Inteligencia Artificial

description: The Bitbrain Open Access Sleep (BOAS) dataset comprises 128 nights of simultaneous polysomnography and wearable EEG recordings from 108 unique participants, designed to validate emerging wearable EEG technology against clinical-grade sleep monitoring standards. The dataset includes human expert consensus sleep stage labels derived from three independent scorers following AASM criteria, as well as AI-generated sleep stage labels from a deep learning model trained on both PSG and wearable EEG data. This dual-recording approach provides a comprehensive resource for evaluating wearable EEG performance in sleep studies and advancing automated sleep staging algorithms.
methods_description: Data were collected simultaneously using a Brain Quick Plus Evolution PSG system (Micromed) and a wearable EEG headband (Bitbrain). PSG recordings included 6-channel EEG (F3, F4, C3, C4, O1, O2), EOG, chin EMG, abdominal and thoracic breathing belts, thermistor and nasal cannula airflow sensors, and PPG-derived pulse, heart rate, and oxygen saturation. The wearable headband recorded forehead EEG (AF7, AF8 approximation), 6-axis IMU data (3-axis accelerometer and gyroscope), and PPG pulse. Sleep stages were independently scored by three expert raters following AASM criteria, with consensus labels derived when at least two raters agreed. A deep learning model was trained using cross-validation on both PSG and wearable EEG datasets, achieving 87.13% and 86.71% agreement with human consensus labels respectively.
